topic: Claude Code skills and MCP tools

claims.claim: Claude Code skills are defined with a SKILL.md file and can be invoked directly or loaded when relevant.
Skill bodies are not always loaded; they load when the skill is used, which makes long procedure content cheaper until needed.
claims.notes: Used in speaker note to explain why deck workflows belong in skills rather than always-on memory.

claims.claim: MCP servers expose tools that clients can invoke on behalf of language models.
claims.notes: Protocol specification used as supporting context for tool-layer claims.

claims.claim: A topic-to-deck workflow should keep source URLs in claim-source-map.json and let slide-spec.json reference only claim ids.
For recurring deck generation, a skill is a better fit than always-on project memory when the content is a multi-step procedure.
claims.notes: Inference based on the official distinction that CLAUDE.md is always-on context while skill content loads when used.

claimsToAvoid.claim: Put every reusable rule into CLAUDE.md.
claimsToAvoid.reason: Always-on memory can become noisy; procedural content should be moved to a skill when it is not needed every turn.
